overview.summary: An astronaut in India is typically employed by ISRO (Indian Space Research Organisation) or international space agencies. The role requires a degree in aerospace/aeronautical engineering, physics, or mathematics, followed by pilot training (often via the Indian Air Force) and rigorous medical/psychological selection. ISRO's Gaganyaan programme and future missions will open recruitment for civilian astronauts. The journey spans 10–15 years from Class 10 completion.
overview.dayInLife: An ISRO astronaut conducts scientific experiments in space, operates spacecraft systems, manages life-support equipment, communicates with mission control, performs spacewalks, and participates in joint international missions. Training involves high-fidelity simulations, physical conditioning, emergency response protocols, and collaboration with international partners.

payRange.note: ISRO compensates based on Grade Pay Level 10–14. International astronauts (NASA/ESA) earn significantly higher. Additional mission bonuses and allowances apply.

overview.demandOutlook: High demand expected. India's Gaganyaan programme (crewed missions starting 2025+) and plans for a space station create opportunities. Private space companies (Skyroot, Agnikul) also hire aerospace engineers. Global space industry growth continues; international agencies recruit Indian talent.
requirements: Bachelor's degree in aerospace/aeronautical engineering, physics, mathematics, or computer science
JEE Main and JEE Advanced qualification for top engineering colleges
Master's degree in aerospace/space engineering preferred
Test pilot certification (especially for ISRO's Gaganyaan track)
Medical fitness, psychological resilience, physical endurance
3–10 years professional experience before astronaut selection

routes.name: ISRO Astronaut Path (IIT/IIST → Aerospace Engineering → IAF Pilot → ISRO Selection)
routes.bestFor: Students aiming for ISRO's crewed missions; those interested in becoming India's first civilian astronauts.
routes.feasibilityReason: This is the most grounded path for Indian aspiring astronauts. IIST and top IITs have proven track records placing graduates at ISRO. The route aligns with ISRO's current astronaut selection focus on IAF test pilots transitioning to space.
routes.duration: 15 years (Class 10 → 12, 4-year BTech, 2-year MSc/MTech, 5-year IAF pilot training, 2+ years ISRO candidacy/selection)

steps.title: Complete Class 10 & Choose Science Stream (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics)
steps.description: Currently in Class 10. At Class 10 board exams, ensure strong performance (aim >85%). Choose Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics (PCM) as mandatory subjects in Class 11. This foundation is non-negotiable for JEE and aerospace careers. Focus on conceptual clarity in physics and maths.

steps.title: Prepare for JEE Main (Classes 11–12)
steps.description: From Class 11 onwards, commit to JEE Main preparation. The exam will be relevant for your engineering college admission. Mock tests, NCERT mastery, and coaching are standard. Aim for a percentile >90 in JEE Main to qualify for JEE Advanced.

steps.title: JEE Main Exam (Sessions 1 & 2)
steps.description: Appear in JEE Main after passing Class 12. Two sessions typically occur in January and April after Class 12 completion. Qualification is mandatory for JEE Advanced and admission to top engineering colleges.

steps.title: JEE Advanced Exam (for IIST/IIT Aerospace)
steps.description: Only top ~250,000 JEE Main qualifiers can sit JEE Advanced. This exam opens doors to IIST Thiruvananthapuram (India's premier space institution) and IIT Aerospace programmes. Aim for All India Rank (AIR) <15,000 for IIST Aerospace Engineering admission.

steps.title: Apply to IIST or Top IIT Aerospace Colleges via JoSAA Counselling
steps.description: Register for JoSAA (Joint Seat Allocation Authority) counselling using JEE Advanced rank. IIST Thiruvananthapuram is the #1 choice for space careers (direct ties to ISRO). IIT Bombay, IIT Madras, IIT Kanpur offer excellent aerospace programmes as alternatives. Lock in your branch preference for Aerospace Engineering or Avionics.

steps.title: B.Tech in Aerospace Engineering (4 years)
steps.description: Complete a rigorous B.Tech in Aerospace Engineering. IIST offers specialised curriculum directly aligned with space missions. Study aerodynamics, propulsion, materials science, spacecraft design, and flight mechanics. Maintain GPA >8/10 (ISRO targets >65% for scientist recruitment). Actively participate in ISRO internship programmes available to IIST students.

steps.title: Dual Degree (B.Tech + M.Tech in Aerospace, 5 years)
steps.description: IIST offers 5-year dual degree programmes combining B.Tech and M.Tech. This accelerates your master's qualification, giving you an edge in ISRO/IAF recruitment. Stronger academic standing for civilian astronaut roles.

steps.title: Intern/Work at ISRO Centres (During BTech, Optional)
steps.description: Apply for ISRO internships during summers (common for IIST/IIT students). Work at VSSC (Vikram Sarabhai Space Centre, Thiruvananthapuram), LPSC (Liquid Propulsion Systems Centre, Tamil Nadu), or other centres. This builds industry experience, exposes you to real spacecraft systems, and creates networking opportunities.

steps.title: Master's Degree in Aerospace/Space Engineering (2 years post-BTech if not dual degree)
steps.description: Pursue an M.Tech in Aerospace Engineering or Space Engineering (IIST, IIT, or equivalent). Thesis/research should focus on areas valued by space agencies: propulsion systems, avionics, thermal management, or mission design. A master's degree is increasingly preferred over bachelor's alone for ISRO scientist roles.

steps.title: Join Indian Air Force (IAF) as Pilot or Direct Engineering Role at ISRO
steps.description: Post-master's, pursue one of two tracks: (A) Join IAF as a fighter/test pilot (National Defence Academy or direct commission). Accumulate 5–10 years of flight experience and 1,000+ pilot-in-command hours on jet aircraft. ISRO prioritises experienced IAF test pilots for astronaut selection. (B) Join ISRO directly as Scientist/Engineer-SC (requires JEE Advanced qualification and interview). Gain 3–5 years of experience in mission design, systems engineering, or operations.

steps.title: Apply for ISRO Astronaut Selection Programme
steps.description: Once you meet experience requirements (typically 3–5 years in relevant role), apply for ISRO's astronaut selection when recruitment opens. ISRO conducts written examinations testing physics, engineering, psychology, and survival skills. Pass candidates undergo rigorous medical tests (fit for long-duration spaceflight), psychological evaluation, physical fitness tests (similar to military), and space simulations.

steps.title: ISRO Astronaut Training & First Mission Assignment
steps.description: Selected astronauts undergo 13+ months of intensive training at ISRO's Human Space Flight Centre (Bengaluru) and potentially at international partners (Russia, ESA). Training includes basic spaceflight operations, spacecraft systems, emergency procedures, survival techniques, EVA (spacewalk) basics, and mission simulations. Post-training, assignment to a Gaganyaan mission or future space station module.

upskilling.name: MATLAB/Simulink for Space Systems
upskilling.why: Essential for modelling spacecraft dynamics, flight simulations, and control systems. Used extensively in ISRO projects.

upskilling.name: CATIA (CAD Software) for Spacecraft Design
upskilling.why: Industry-standard for aerospace component design. ISRO and Airbus use CATIA for mission-critical parts.

upskilling.name: Russian Language Basics
upskilling.why: India partners with Russia (Gaganyaan training centres). Speaking Russian is a competitive advantage for international training.

upskilling.name: High-Altitude/Survival Training Certification
upskilling.why: Military-grade courses prepare you for ejection drills, altitude physiology, survival in harsh environments. Some offered by IAF.

upskilling.name: GATE Exam Preparation (for PG Admission & ISRO Recruitment)
upskilling.why: GATE is used for post-graduate engineering admission at IIST and ISRO scientist recruitment in some cases. High score strengthens candidacy.

upskilling.name: Online Aerospace Specialisation (MIT OpenCourseWare, edX, Coursera)
upskilling.why: MIT, Stanford, and ISRO offer free or low-cost courses in orbital mechanics, propulsion, avionics. Strengthen fundamentals and credentials.

upskilling.name: SCUBA Certification & Water Survival Training
upskilling.why: Astronauts train in water to simulate weightlessness. SCUBA and pool rescue certs are preparatory.

upskilling.name: Leadership & Teamwork Soft Skills Coaching
upskilling.why: ISRO's psychological selection emphasises communication, crisis management, and interpersonal skills. Coaching helps you stand out.

exams.name: JEE Main 2027
exams.purpose: Entrance exam for engineering colleges in India. Mandatory to qualify for JEE Advanced and admissions to NITs, IITs, and IIST.
exams.eligibility: Class 12 pass (or appearing) with Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics. Must pass Class 12 in 2026 or 2027.
exams.typicalWindow: Applications typically open ~October 2026 for Session 1 (January 2027); Session 2 window ~February 2027 for April exam. Verify on jeemain.nta.nic.in.

exams.name: JEE Advanced 2027
exams.purpose: Advanced entrance exam for IITs, IIST, and deemed universities. Only top ~250,000 JEE Main qualifiers are eligible. Admission to aerospace engineering programmes at IIST and IITs depends on JEE Advanced rank.
exams.eligibility: Must qualify JEE Main 2027 and rank within top 250,000. Registration typically opens ~2–3 weeks after JEE Main Session 2 results.
exams.typicalWindow: Registrations usually open ~April/May 2027; exam held ~May/June 2027. Verify on jeeadv.ac.in for exact dates.

exams.name: GATE (Graduate Aptitude Test in Engineering) 2028/2029+
exams.purpose: Required for M.Tech admission at IIST and other institutions. Also used by ISRO for some scientist recruitment. Strengthens your candidacy.
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering or science. Typically taken after BTech completion or during final year.
exams.typicalWindow: Applications usually open ~September–October; exam held ~January–February. Check gate.iisc.ac.in.

exams.name: ISRO Scientist/Engineer Recruitment Exam (Periodic)
exams.purpose: Direct entry into ISRO as Scientist/Engineer-SC. Tests technical knowledge (physics, maths, engineering) and aptitude. Only open to JEE Advanced qualifiers or top graduates.
exams.eligibility: BTech/ME in aerospace, mechanical, electrical, electronics, or related fields with >65% marks (or CGPA 6.84/10). JEE Advanced qualification required.
exams.typicalWindow: ISRO announces recruitment periodically (no fixed schedule). Notifications appear on https://www.isro.gov.in/. Typically registration ~2–4 weeks.

exams.name: ISRO Astronaut Selection Programme (Periodic, Gaganyaan Era)
exams.purpose: Final selection to become an ISRO astronaut. Includes written exam, psychological testing, medical screening (similar to military standards), physical fitness (VO2 max, endurance), and space simulations.
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree (or Master's preferred) in aerospace/aeronautical engineering, physics, mathematics, or computer science. 3+ years professional experience (ISRO, IAF, or research). Fluent English.
exams.typicalWindow: ISRO opens recruitment sporadically, typically when preparing for new crewed missions (e.g., Gaganyaan). Check https://www.isro.gov.in/careers for notifications. No fixed schedule; can be years apart.

colleges.name: Indian Institute of Space Science and Technology (IIST)
colleges.approxAnnualFees: ₹2,71,800–3,15,000 (BTech, depending on family income; SC/ST tuition-free)
colleges.feesNote: Highly subsidised fees compared to private colleges. Income-based fee structure available. Merit scholarships cover full/partial fees for top performers.

colleges.name: IIT Bombay (Aerospace Engineering)
colleges.feesNote: Moderate government fees. Excellent aerospace programme with placements at ISRO, Airbus, HAL.

colleges.name: IIT Madras (Aerospace Engineering)
colleges.feesNote: Competitive aerospace programme. Strong research facilities and ISRO collaborations.

colleges.name: IIT Kanpur (Aerospace Engineering)
colleges.feesNote: Strong aerospace lab and research focus. Median package >₹22 LPA for aerospace graduates.

colleges.name: IIT Kharagpur (Aerospace Engineering)
colleges.feesNote: One of India's oldest aerospace programmes. Strong core sector placements (DRDO, ISRO, HAL).

colleges.name: RV College of Engineering (Bangalore)
colleges.feesNote: Well-regarded private college with aerospace programme. Placements include Airbus, Bosch, TCS.
colleges.entranceRequired: JEE Main (cut-off typically >95 percentile for aerospace)

colleges.name: SRM Institute of Science and Technology (Chennai)
colleges.feesNote: Strong aerospace programme with modern labs. Average placements ₹7.5–10 LPA.

colleges.name: Amrita School of Engineering (Coimbatore)
colleges.feesNote: Well-resourced aerospace programme. Industry partnerships with global space companies.
colleges.entranceRequired: JEE Main or Amrita Entrance Exam

colleges.name: Manipal Institute of Technology (Karnataka)
colleges.feesNote: Reputable private college with aerospace specialisation. Dual degree option available.
colleges.entranceRequired: JEE Main or Manipal Entrance Test

colleges.name: Lovely Professional University (Punjab)
colleges.feesNote: Affordable private option with aerospace engineering. Good infrastructure and industry tie-ups.
colleges.entranceRequired: JEE Main or LPU NEST entrance

options: If you miss JEE Main 2027 Session 1, apply in Session 2 (typically April). JEE Main allows attempts in up to 3 consecutive years; you have until 2029 to qualify.

If JEE Main/JEE Advanced scores fall short of IIST/IIT aerospace cutoffs, consider excellent private aerospace colleges (SRM, Manipal, Amrita, Lovely Professional University). Private colleges admit via JEE Main cutoffs, often allowing ~85–90 percentile entry.
Alternative entry: Some colleges offer BTech via direct merit (HSC marks + entrance test). Contact colleges for direct schemes.
If you cannot pursue BTech immediately, take a gap year to prepare for JEE Main 2028. GATE can also be used 2–3 years post-graduation for M.Tech entry and ISRO roles.
Consider international universities (if affordable) offering aerospace degrees recognised for ISRO recruitment (e.g., Technion, University of Stuttgart). However, this delays your path by 1–2 years.

routes.name: Aerospace Research & Development Path (Physics Degree → Master's → ISRO Scientist via GATE)
routes.bestFor: Students interested in research, theoretical astrophysics, or propulsion systems design; alternative to engineering track.
routes.feasibilityReason: Less direct than the engineering path but viable. ISRO recruits PhDs and Master's holders in physics. Requires strong fundamentals and research orientation. Better suited if you prefer pure science over hands-on engineering.
routes.duration: 12–14 years (Class 10 → 12, 3-year BSc Physics, 2-year MSc, PhD research 3–4 years, 2+ years ISRO candidacy)

steps.title: Complete Class 10 & Choose Science (Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics)
steps.description: Currently in Class 10. Ensure strong performance in board exams. Choose PCM for Class 11–12.

steps.title: Bachelor's Degree in Physics (3 years)
steps.description: After Class 12, enrol in a BSc Honours Physics programme from a top university (Delhi University, Bombay University, IISc Bangalore, or IIST). Focus on optics, mechanics, thermodynamics, and modern physics. Maintain >75% marks (minimum for later ISRO/PhD eligibility).

steps.title: GATE Physics Exam (Final or post-BSc year)
steps.description: Write GATE Physics to secure a strong rank (top 500 preferred) for M.Tech admission and fellowship support at top institutes.

steps.title: M.Tech in Aerospace/Space Engineering or PhD in Physics (2–4 years)
steps.description: Pursue M.Tech in Space Engineering or Aerospace (via GATE score) at IIST, IIT, or equivalent. Or transition directly to PhD in Physics (condensed matter, astrophysics, or space plasma) at IISER, IIT, or university. PhD is preferred for ISRO scientist recruitment in research domains.

steps.title: Research Internships at ISRO Centres (During M.Tech/PhD)
steps.description: Apply for ISRO Visiting Student Research Programme (VSRP) or post-doctoral fellowships. Work at IUCAA (Inter-University Centre for Astronomy and Astrophysics), SDSC, or VSSC on space science projects.

steps.title: ISRO Scientist (Senior Research Fellow/Postdoc) Recruitment
steps.description: Apply for ISRO's Scientist/Engineer recruitment (for PhDs and MTechs). Roles include space scientist, propulsion researcher, astrophysicist. Requires strong publication record and research experience.

steps.title: Transition to Astronaut Selection (if desired)
steps.description: As an ISRO scientist with research credentials, you become eligible for astronaut selection when ISRO opens civilian recruitment. Your physics/research background is valued for mission science and systems knowledge, especially for long-duration missions.

coreSkills: Advanced physics (mechanics, electromagnetism, quantum theory, astrophysics)

upskilling.name: Python for Scientific Computing (NumPy, SciPy, Matplotlib)
upskilling.why: Essential for astrophysics simulations, data analysis, and ISRO research projects.

upskilling.name: Fortran Programming (for Legacy ISRO Code)
upskilling.why: Much of ISRO's legacy scientific software is in Fortran. Knowledge gives you an edge in research roles.

upskilling.name: Orbital Mechanics & Celestial Mechanics
upskilling.why: Core skill for mission design and space navigation. Offered via Coursera, MIT OpenCourseWare.

upskilling.name: GATE Physics Preparation Course
upskilling.why: Strengthens candidacy for M.Tech admission and ISRO recruitment. Many online coaches available.

exams.name: GATE Physics 2029 (Post-BSc)
exams.purpose: Gateway exam for M.Tech admission and ISRO scientist recruitment. Scores are valid for 3 years.
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree (or final year) in Physics or related science. No age limit.

exams.name: JEST (Joint Entrance Screening Test) 2029+ (For PhD Admission)
exams.purpose: Entrance exam for PhD programmes in Physics at IISER, IIT, IISC. Accepted by ISRO for research fellowships.
exams.eligibility: Bachelor's degree in Physics or related field.

exams.name: ISRO Scientist Recruitment Exam (Periodic, SC Category for Scientists)
exams.purpose: Direct recruitment for ISRO scientist roles (not astronaut yet). Focuses on research aptitude and subject knowledge.
exams.eligibility: PhD or M.Tech in relevant field (aerospace, physics, mechanical engineering) with strong academic record.

colleges.name: Indian Institute of Science (IISc) Bangalore
colleges.feesNote: Excellence-based admission; very competitive. Strong physics and space science research.
colleges.entranceRequired: KVPY (Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana) or direct interview

colleges.name: Indian Institute of Science Education and Research (IISER) Pune
colleges.feesNote: Premier institute for research-oriented science. Direct entry via KVPY or JEE Advanced.

colleges.name: Delhi University (Physics Honours)
colleges.feesNote: Strong physics programme; many ISRO scientists are DU alumni.

colleges.name: Bombay University (Physics)
colleges.feesNote: Excellent physics research facilities. ISRO tie-ups for internships.

options: If GATE Physics scores fall short, apply for direct PhD admission via JEST (Joint Entrance Screening Test) or institutional entrance exams.
Take a gap year and retake GATE (valid for 3 years; you can sit multiple times).
Apply for M.Tech programmes via direct merit (BSc marks + interview) at universities.
Consider postdoctoral fellowships at ISRO or international institutes post-PhD; path to astronaut selection via research contributions.

routes.name: Pilot Track → Astronaut (IAF Pilot → ISRO Selection)
routes.bestFor: Students with strong interest in aviation and flying; can start BTech and transition to IAF pilot training.
routes.feasibilityReason: ISRO currently prioritises experienced IAF test pilots (the four Gaganyaan astronauts are all test pilots). However, this route requires IAF commission and multi-year pilot experience before astronaut selection. Less direct than pure aerospace engineering but aligned with ISRO's current selection focus.
routes.duration: 14–16 years (Class 10 → 12, BTech/Physics degree, IAF pilot training 3–4 years, 5–10 years flight experience, astronaut selection)

steps.title: Complete Class 12 with Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics
steps.description: Currently in Class 10. Prepare for Class 12 board exams; aim for >85% in PCM. Register for NDA (National Defence Academy) entrance exam.

steps.title: Union Public Service Commission (UPSC) NDA Entrance Exam
steps.description: Write UPSC NDA exam (conducted twice yearly). Clear written test, then face SSB (Service Selection Board) interview. NDA is the primary gateway to IAF pilot commission.

steps.title: National Defence Academy (NDA) Training (2 years)
steps.description: If selected, complete 2-year residential NDA training at Khadakwasla (Pune). Training covers military discipline, academics, flying basics, physical fitness. Post-NDA, commission as Flying Officer in IAF.

steps.title: IAF Flying Training (Fighter Pilot Training Programme, 3–4 years)
steps.description: Undergo IAF's Flying Training Course at flying schools (Dundigal, Bidar, etc.). Train on aircraft like HAL Tejas, Hawk trainer. Earn your pilot wings. Goal: accumulate 1,000+ hours of pilot-in-command time on jet aircraft (needed for ISRO astronaut eligibility).

steps.title: Operational Flying with IAF (5–10 years)
steps.description: Fly operational fighter or transport aircraft missions with IAF squadrons. Build experience, flight hours, and crisis management skills. Test pilot training can begin after 3–5 years of operational flying.

steps.title: Test Pilot Training (Optional but Recommended for Astronaut Selection)
steps.description: Apply for Test Pilot Training at IAF's Test Pilot School. Become a certified test pilot. Test pilot experience is what ISRO prioritises for astronaut selection (all four Gaganyaan astronauts are test pilots).

steps.title: Apply for ISRO Astronaut Selection Programme
steps.description: With test pilot credentials and 5–10+ years flight experience, apply when ISRO announces astronaut recruitment. Your IAF background and flight expertise make you a strong candidate (priority track).

coreSkills: Piloting skills (aircraft handling, navigation, emergency procedures)
Technical aptitude for avionics and flight systems

upskilling.name: Ground School for Jet Aircraft Systems (IAF-provided)
upskilling.why: IAF training includes comprehensive ground school on fighter aircraft systems, propulsion, avionics.

upskilling.name: Aerospace Engineering Basics (Self-study)
upskilling.why: Understanding aeronautics, propulsion, and flight dynamics enhances your candidacy as an astronaut.

upskilling.name: Survival & Leadership Training (IAF-provided)
upskilling.why: Core IAF training covers survival, leadership, and psychological resilience needed for astronauts.

upskilling.name: Russian Language (For Astronaut Training Abroad)
upskilling.why: ISRO astronauts train in Russia. Speaking Russian is a bonus.

exams.name: UPSC NDA (National Defence Academy) Entrance Exam
exams.purpose: Gateway to IAF officer commission. Conducted twice yearly by UPSC. Clear written exam, then face SSB (Service Selection Board) interview.
exams.eligibility: Class 12 pass (or appearing). Indian citizen. Age 16–19 years at time of exam.
exams.typicalWindow: Typically applications open ~April and September. Exams held ~May and November. Check upsc.gov.in.

exams.name: IAF Pilot Medical Exam & SSB Interview
exams.purpose: Medical fitness and psychological assessment for pilot suitability. Conducted after NDA written exam clearance.

colleges.name: National Defence Academy (NDA) Khadakwasla
colleges.approxAnnualFees: Free (fully government-funded; cadet receives salary during training)
colleges.feesNote: All expenses covered. Cadets receive monthly stipend (~₹21,000+) during 2-year training.

colleges.name: IAF Flying Training Schools (Dundigal, Bidar, Hakimpet, etc.)
colleges.approxAnnualFees: Free (fully funded by IAF; officer receives salary)
colleges.feesNote: Officers receive salary + allowances during training. No tuition fees.

options: If NDA interview fails, retake the exam. NDA is held twice yearly; multiple attempts possible until age 19.
Alternative: Pursue BTech in Physics/Aerospace Engineering while applying to NDA; if IAF path doesn't work, you have an engineering degree for ISRO scientist roles.
Once past age 19, apply for IAF's Direct Entry Scheme (for graduate engineers). Shorter path (~3 years to pilot wings) but less preferred by ISRO initially.
Consider civilian aviation routes: Commercial pilot licence (CPL) → airlines. However, this doesn't lead to astronaut selection (ISRO wants military test pilots).

prepResources.title: MIT OpenCourseWare: Orbital Mechanics and Astrodynamics
Coursera: Physics & Astronomy Specialisation (Johns Hopkins University)
JEE Main Official Practice Papers & Syllabus (NTA)
"An Introduction to the Space Environment" by Paul M. Withers (Free PDF, MIT)
ISRO Official Webpage & Gaganyaan Mission Documentation
YouTube Channel: ISRO Official (Lectures, Mission Insights, Recruitment Info)
Unacademy: JEE Main & Advanced Preparation (Free trials available)
Physics Wallah: NCERT Physics & Maths (Free YouTube channel)

groundingSources.claim: ISRO astronaut eligibility: Bachelor's degree in engineering, physics, or maths; post-graduation in aerospace preferred; Indian citizenship.
Gaganyaan astronauts (4) selected in Feb 2024 are all experienced IAF test pilots.
ISRO medical/psychological/physical/simulation tests are mandatory for astronaut selection.
JEE Main 2027 expected to be held in 2 sessions (January & April 2027); application window ~October–November 2026 for Session 1.
JEE Advanced registration typically opens ~2–3 weeks after JEE Main results; conducted in May/June.
JEE Advanced 2026 exam held on May 17, 2026; registration concluded May 5, 2026.
NEET registration 2026 closed on March 11, 2026; exam held May 3, 2026.
IIST BTech fees ₹2.71–3.15 lakh/year (income-based); MTech ₹40,450/semester; JEE Advanced required for admission.
IIST ranked 61st NIRF Engineering 2025; offers Aerospace Engineering, Avionics, Engineering Physics.
IIT Bombay, IIT Madras, IIT Kanpur, IIT Kharagpur are top aerospace engineering colleges; BTech ~₹1.43 lakh/year.
JEE Main syllabus covers Physics, Chemistry, Mathematics from NCERT Class 11–12 curriculum.
ISRO scientist recruitment requires BTech/MTech with minimum 65% marks or CGPA 6.84/10; JEE Advanced qualification needed.
IAF test pilot experience and 1,000+ pilot-in-command hours are required for ISRO astronaut selection.
UPSC NDA exam conducted twice yearly; eligibility age 16–19 years; gateway to IAF pilot commission.

disclaimers: This career journey is based on currently available information as of June 2026. ISRO's astronaut selection criteria, exam schedules, and fee structures may change. Always verify details on official websites (isro.gov.in, jeemain.nta.nic.in, jeeadv.ac.in, iist.ac.in, indianairforce.nic.in) before making decisions.

Astronaut selection is highly competitive. While this roadmap outlines the pathway, acceptance into ISRO's astronaut programme is not guaranteed and depends on merit, medical fitness, psychological evaluation, and mission requirements.
Fees listed are approximate and subject to revision by colleges and boards. Income-based fee waivers and scholarships may apply; contact colleges directly for current information.

JEE exam dates, registration windows, and application forms are announced by NTA/IIT periodically. Verify exact dates and links on official portals; this document reflects typical schedules and may not reflect real-time changes.

The 'offsetMonths' values are relative timelines from today (June 19, 2026) and should be cross-checked against your actual academic calendar. Regional variations (state board differences, college-specific schedules) may apply.

International astronaut routes (NASA, ESA) are not covered in detail here; they typically require higher qualifications and US/EU citizenship. Consult international space agencies' websites for those pathways.
Private space company roles (Skyroot, Agnikul, etc.) are emerging alternatives; they may offer astronaut or astronaut-adjacent career paths not detailed in this document.
Verified: false means that specific details (fee amounts, exact exam dates, college cutoffs) were not directly verified by the user on official sources. Always double-check before commitment.
